title: astragalus lambertii var. bigelovii
intro: Astragalus lambertii var. bigelovii, also known as Bigelow's milkvetch, is a type of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae), native to the western United States and Canada. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. Astragalus lambertii var. bigelovii is believed to have various health benefits, such as boosting the immune system, reducing stress and fatigue, and potentially lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, capsules, and extracts, which are often used to support overall health and well-being.
